summary: Verified US-845 desktop table accessibility for keyboard/action usability and assistive-technology-readable semantics. Spexus current_context was read before task and epic artifacts. Dependency reports for MZ-501, MZ-502, MZ-503, and the prior US-844 accessibility precedent were present and successful. Source and tests show legal actions, start controls, and playable cards use native buttons; ineligible cards and pending discard confirmation are disabled; discard toggles expose aria-pressed and stateful aria labels; finished bullets expose no continuation controls; focus-visible styles remain present for start controls, actions, secondary controls, and cards. State, score, deal-result, and final-result surfaces expose visible headings, labeled sections, dl/dt/dd metadata, native score-change table semantics, result headings, card/action labels, and visible active actor/phase text. Scope was kept to desktop/laptop usability, with full mobile accessibility correctly not required. Targeted tests and the release gate passed.

tests_run.evidence: 3 test files passed, 35 tests passed. Covered renderer accessibility semantics, legal-only action projection, discard selection labels/state, focus-order-sensitive start/resume flow, and representative US-845 desktop table states.
Vitest passed 14 files / 134 tests, then tsc and vite build completed successfully.

verification_environment.verification_scope: US-845 accessibility review for desktop/laptop table UI states, action/card keyboard usability, state/score/result semantics, and release-gate readiness using source inspection plus existing Vitest/jsdom and build entrypoints

node_package_scripts.verify: npm run test && npm run build

verification_environment.notes: No production source files were changed. Verification did not require a full mobile accessibility audit because the task explicitly scopes accessibility review to desktop/laptop usability.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Desktop table shows current playable state with phase, active actor, legal human actions, player hands, score context, result context, contract/trump/trick context where applicable, and no misleading controls.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts renderGame/stateContextMarkup/renderActions/humanCards/tableContent/scoreboardMarkup/renderDealResultPanel/renderFinalResultPanel; src/ui/render.test.ts; src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts; npm run test -- src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts; npm run verify
evidence_matrix.limitations: Verification uses source inspection and jsdom DOM assertions, not a real screen-reader transcript or browser tab-walk recording.

evidence_matrix.scenario: At desktop/laptop scope, bidding, contract/order, widow/discard, whist/check, contract play, all-pass, misere, deal-result, and final-result states remain keyboard-usable and understandable.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts actionButton/setupDiscardSelection/humanCards/stateContextMarkup; src/styles.css focus-visible rules; src/ui/render.test.ts legal controls, discard labels, accessible names, card enablement, finished-state, AI-turn tests; src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts representative desktop-state tests
evidence_matrix.limitations: Desktop/laptop usability is verified through deterministic DOM states and CSS source review; full mobile accessibility is outside this task's scope.

evidence_matrix.scenario: After a deal completes, result and next-deal states remain readable and expose a valid proceed action only when the bullet is not complete; completed bullets expose final results without continuation controls.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts renderDealResultPanel/renderFinalResultPanel/actionNotice/renderActions; src/ui/render.test.ts structured deal result, next-deal action, final no-continuation tests; src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts deal settlement, next-deal, final result tests; npm run verify
evidence_matrix.limitations: Dynamic announcement via aria-live is not present, consistent with prior US-844 precedent; the rendered static result state is structured and labeled.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Controls reachable by keyboard are native buttons, focus-visible styles are present, resume keyboard order puts continue before start-new choices, and AI turns/final states avoid inaccessible or misleading controls.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts renderStartScreen/actionButton/humanCards/renderActions/setupDiscardSelection; src/styles.css .start-size:focus-visible, .start-continue:focus-visible, .actions button:focus-visible, .secondary:focus-visible, button.card:focus-visible; src/ui/controller.test.ts resume keyboard order; src/ui/render.test.ts AI-turn/final/no-action tests
evidence_matrix.limitations: No browser-level tab traversal automation exists in project scripts; reachability is verified through native button DOM order and enabled/disabled state assertions.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Legal action controls and playable cards expose accessible names; discard card toggles communicate selected/unselected state and confirmation remains disabled until a legal pair is selected.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts actionAccessibleLabel/cardLabel/humanCards/renderContractActions/setupDiscardSelection; src/ui/render.test.ts labels discard card toggles with selection state and a clear confirmation name; provides accessible names for legal action controls and playable cards; keeps discard confirmation disabled until a legal pair is selected
evidence_matrix.limitations: Play-card action buttons use card labels for visible human cards; actionAccessibleLabel for programmatic playCard is not used on those card buttons.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Assistive technologies can read state context, score panel, deal result, and final result through headings, aria-labelledby sections, dl/dt/dd metadata, table headers, ordered ranking, and visible active actor/phase text.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts scoreboardMarkup/stateContextMarkup/renderDealResultPanel/renderFinalResultPanel/status-line; src/ui/render.test.ts compact score panel, structured result details, result-cell labels, final ranking tests; src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts score/state/result checks
evidence_matrix.limitations: The compact current score panel uses labeled sections and definition lists rather than one literal score table, matching the accepted US-844 accessibility precedent.

evidence_matrix.scenario: The implementation under verification delivered the desktop state context and result/score surfaces now reviewed for accessibility.
evidence_matrix.limitations: This verifier did not change production code; findings are based on inspecting the shipped MZ-502 implementation and test evidence.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Representative desktop state families are covered by integration tests for bidding, contract-family, play, all-pass, misere, settlement, next-deal, and final-result states.
evidence_matrix.limitations: Integration coverage is jsdom/in-process renderer evidence rather than browser viewport automation.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts native button rendering; src/styles.css focus-visible rules; src/ui/controller.test.ts resume keyboard order; src/ui/render.test.ts legal action, card enablement, AI-turn, final-state tests
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Focus order is verified by DOM order and native button behavior, not by browser tab automation.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts actionButton/actionAccessibleLabel/renderContractActions; src/ui/render.test.ts provides accessible names for legal action controls and playable cards; labels discard confirmation
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Only currently rendered legal human actions are exposed as controls; AI-turn controls are intentionally absent.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts humanCards/cardLabel/setupDiscardSelection; src/ui/render.test.ts accessible names for playable cards and discard toggle state
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Opponent card backs are aria-hidden decorative spans inside opponent hand containers; hidden opponent card identities are intentionally not exposed.
No aria-live announcement is implemented for result transitions; prior US-844 precedent accepted static labeled result structures for this app flow.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: npm run test -- src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts; npm run verify
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Regression evidence is local deterministic Vitest/jsdom plus build verification.
Release readiness is scoped to the repository's current test/build scripts in this worktree.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: PERSONAL-TASK-1164 scope; docs/test-design/us-845-desktop-table-states.md; src/styles.css desktop table layout and focus styles
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: This accessibility review covers desktop/laptop usability. Full mobile accessibility and screenshot-diff visual QA are not required by this task.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: PERSONAL-TASK-1164 NFR classification; src/ui/render.ts; src/styles.css; targeted tests
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: This verification covers local UI accessibility only and touches no auth, backend, network, storage schema, or sensitive-data path.
